q: Electrical faults in appliances or circuits can cause fires, electric shocks, as well as ___ to users.
Either high current or high ___ can be dangerous to human.
The dangers of electricity can be traced to three possible causes: damaged insulation, overheating of cables, and ___ conditions.
All electrical appliances require two wires, the live and ___ wires, to form a complete circuit.
The live wire is the dangerous wire because it carries a ___ voltage.
The neutral wire is close to ___ voltage.
Live and neutral wires are insulated in ___ rubber.
Insulating materials ___ with time and use.
Damaged electrical insulation can cause the electrical insulation to ___ and break.
If the vulcanised rubber covering the live wire is damaged, the exposed live wire can cause a severe ___ to the user.
A severe electric shock can lead to serious injury and even ___.
The overheating of cables is due to the extremely large current flowing under conditions such as a short circuit or ___.
A short circuit can result when the live wire makes electrical contact with the neutral wire due to ___ insulation.
A short circuit produces a large current and large amount of heat, which can melt insulation and start a ___.
Overuse of extension cords and multiple plug adapters on the same circuit are typical causes of an electrical ___.
Overload occurs when an equipment draws an ___ current from the supply.
Many electrical accidents occur in ___ conditions in a wet bathroom.
If water from the wet floor touches the live wire, it provides a conducting path for current, exposing the person to the danger of ___.
The human body can only withstand up to about ___ mA of current.
The current will be much higher due to the sharp decrease in the resistance of the ___ body.
Other possible hazards include using hair dryers, electric irons or changing a light bulb with ___ hands.
A current of 1 mA corresponds to the Threshold effect, where there is ___ pain.
A current of 5 mA causes a frightening but ___ shock.
A current of 10-20 mA causes uncontrolled muscular ___.
A current of 50 mA causes pain and exhaustion, with ___ affected.
A current of 100-300 mA causes uncoordinated contraction of the heart leading to ___.
One should never operate an electrical appliance with ___ hands.
Switches, plugs, sockets, connecting wires, etc. should always be in a ___ condition.
The amount of current flowing through the body determines whether you receive an electrical ___.
Overheating of cables is caused by extremely large current flowing due to a short circuit or ___.
